title: wendlandia proxima
intro: Wendlandia proxima, also known as the Chinese box leaf or Chinese boxwood, is a plant species in the Rubiaceae family that is native to southern China and northern India. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Wendlandia proxima include reducing fever, relieving headaches and body aches, and treating respiratory infections, such as bronchitis and pneumonia. Herbal preparations of Wendlandia proxima include decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by boiling the leaves in water or mixing them with a solvent like ethanol.
